observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to conduct a comprehensive annual physical inventory of its properties, and prepare and submit the RPCPPE in accordance with Section 124, of MNGAS for LGUs Volume 1.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the GSO agreed to prepare in the prescribed format their respective PPE Ledger Cards, Real Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, and perform a reconciliation of the reported PPEs per physical count, accounting and property records to establish the correct balance of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

Lastly, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure proper recording of PPE in the books, proper computation and reporting of the related depreciation expense, comply with the recognition requirement for tangible items that fall below the capitalization threshold of ₱50,000.00 as stipulated in COA Circular No. 2022-004, and make the necessary journal entries to effect the required adjustments, to make certain the accuracy and reliability of the reported PPE balances and achieve fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Officer-in-Charge of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to ensure the propriety of the certified list of RPT and SET due and collectible for the year that is prepared based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Index Card from the Assessor’s Office showing the true and accurate value of receivables, and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year for the setting up of receivables.

We likewise recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to henceforth, recognize in the books the total RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based solely on the certified list of due and collectible real property taxes submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, and scrutinize the propriety of the report before recording, in order to properly account for the total real property tax receivables of the year.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly prepare the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2016-004 and 2012-002 and make certain the propriety and completeness of required disclosures to achieve fair presentation of financial statements.


We also recommended and the Acting Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile the balances appearing in their respective reports to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the balances reflected in the Financial Statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer’s Office agreed to submit immediately the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers with supporting documents, and cancelled checks to the Office of the Auditor and to strictly adhere to the timeframes on the rendition of accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 and other pertinent issuances.

We recommended and the MDRRMO agreed to maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF to strengthen the Municipality’s disaster risk reduction and management capability, prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the prescribed format and content, and to prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Receipts and Utilization of the DRRM Fund in accordance with the provisions of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73.

We likewise recommended and the Management agreed to refrain from charging against the LDRRMF expenditure items that are not DRRM related, and instead provide budgetary support to the MDRRM Office for these expenditure items which should be covered by the general fund of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to impose payment of liquidated damages to suppliers/contractors of the listed goods and infrastructure projects with delayed delivery/ completion, in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
We likewise recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the warranty securities for equipment procured, and henceforth, ensure that goods procured are covered by warranty security, payments for progress billings are withheld with retention money, and liquidated damages are imposed to suppliers/contractors with delayed delivery/ project completion, in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We also recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to submit the required listing of materials representing 4.7 percent of delayed works to substantiate the delayed completion of the provision of potable water system project; submit the itemized cost of revision/extra work with explanation for the construction of perimeter fence of old dumping facility project; and to henceforth, make certain that variation orders are substantiated, to ensure the propriety of claims.

We recommended and the OIC of Municipal Treasurer’s Office agreed 
to:

Intensify its collection efforts for the uncollected market stall rentals;



Ensure the accuracy of the submitted report on market stall rental dues, collections and balances for proper monitoring and timely decision making; and

Submit the lacking official receipts showing collections from market stall rentals to establish the total amount collected during the year.

We recommended and Management agreed to constitute a Disposal Committee to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 379 of RA No. 7160 and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386, as updated by COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2024-1 dated January 30, 2024.

We recommended and the MENRO and concerned personnel agreed to:

Provide assistance to the barangays in the     management of their solid waste and actively    promote and enforce the sorting and segregation of wastes at the household level and all other sources to ensure that only non-recyclable materials and special wastes are collected by the Municipality for proper and efficient storage in its facilities, pursuant to pertinent provisions of RA No. 9003; and

Establish a policy for the generation of revenue from saleable recyclables, ensure that collections are properly remitted to the Municipality, and expenditures undergo the proper authorization and accounting of disbursements from public funds.

Fully implement infrastructure projects relative to solid waste management and prepare a comprehensive work and financial plan relative to solid waste management operations to establish the timing and extent of implementation of each program, project and activities during the year and to employ monitoring mechanisms to ensure full implementation thereof.

We recommended and the GAD Focal Person and concerned offices agreed to maximize the utilization of the GAD Fund through implementation of programs and projects listed in the GAD Plan and Budget, to ensure that identified priority gender issues are fully addressed and gender mainstreaming is completely achieved.

We also recommended and the GAD Focal Person agreed to henceforth, ensure the submission to the Auditor of the supporting schedules providing complete details on the list of expenditures reported in the GAD AR to enable the proper review and determination of the propriety of expenses charged against the GAD Fund.

We recommended and Management agreed for the:

GSO to ensure that issuance of supplies and materials are supported with Requisition and Issue Slip, and prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued to the Municipal Accountant for the recording of expense;

Inventory Committee to conduct semestral physical count of inventories to determine the undistributed/ unconsumed items, prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI), and reconcile the same to the recorded inventories of the Accounting Office; and

Municipal Accountant to ensure that the recognition of the expenses relative to the issuance of inventories are duly supported with Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued accompanied by Requisition and Issue Slips.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and OIC of Municipal Treasurer’s Office agreed to secure a copy of the Certificate of Time Deposits and documents evidencing that prior authority and approval from the Sangguniang Bayan and the Local Chief Executive was taken, including other documents to prove the existence of the investment to time deposits, and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor for review.

We recommended and Management has agreed to direct the BAC Chairman and her concerned personnel to submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor, the lacking documents to support the negotiated procurement of various ancillary supplies for the COVID-19 vaccination program and henceforth, to adhere strictly with the provisions of RA No. 9184 in the conduct of its procurement activities.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the fund transfers received from source agencies are supported with duly signed Memorandum of Agreement and related documents, before allowing any disbursements and make certain that funds are utilized only for its intended purpose.

We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Focal Person/Project Proponent Department Heads to implement immediately without delay the funded programs of various LGUs and NGAs to attain the program’s objectives.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and concerned offices agreed to submit the lacking documents to the Office of the Auditor, and validate the discrepancies noted in the reported liquidation in comparison with the duly signed payrolls to ascertain the propriety of the usage of fund.

We recommended and the GAD Focal Person agreed to prepare the Municipality’s GAD Agenda for the three-year period as required under Item C2 par. 1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to establish the Municipality’s strategic framework and plan on gender mainstreaming, women empowerment and gender equality; and prepare the annual GPB in accordance with the GAD Agenda to ensure that identified programs and projects will answer GAD issues that directly relates to the GAD goals of the Municipality for the year.

We further recommended and the GAD Focal Person, the Municipal Accountant and other concerned offices agreed to establish a sound system of monitoring in the recording of expenditures and utilization of the Fund and make periodic reconciliation to ensure correctness of reports submitted.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
 
Require the previous Municipal Treasurer to produce the undeposited collections and deposit the same with the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB) and provide explanation why the said amount was undeposited as of December 31, 2021 or if necessary, initiate proper proceedings; and

Require the current OIC - Municipal Treasurer to verify and reconcile their records with the reflected amount in the FS prepared by the Accounting and to deposit intact all the collections with the AGDB daily or not later than the next banking day as required under Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Section 32 of MNGAS for LGUs Volume I.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned Officials and Employees to submit thru the personnel concerned the Certificate of Attendance during ECQ/MECQ, daily time record/logbook and Sangguniang Bayan Resolution determining the amount to be granted to each employee taking into consideration LGUs financial capability.

We recommended and the management through the LCE agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant and OIC- Municipal Treasurer submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor, the unsubmitted DVs together with its corresponding supporting documents and liquidation reports for the period January 1 to December 31, 2021; and henceforth, to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to:

Require the liquidation of all unliquidated cash advances, otherwise initiate withholding of salaries pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to monitor cash advances and refrain from granting additional cash advances to those with unsettled accounts pursuant to Section 89 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the officials/employees concerned to submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor, the lacking documents to support the negotiated procurement of various supplies for COVID-19 relief operations and henceforth, to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of GPPB Resolution No. 03-2020, Republic Act No. 9184 and COA Circular 2012-001.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the concerned personnel to submit immediately the Monthly Report of Official Travels with the corresponding Monthly Report of Consumption of Fuel, Oil and Lubricants for each vehicle for CY 2020 to the Audit Team for verification and determination of the reasonableness of the charges made to Fuel, Oil & Lubricants Expenses and henceforth, to submit the said reports within a reasonable period every month thereafter.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enforce full settlement of the calendar year 2015 disallowances pursuant to the prescribed Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts as provided in Section 7.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Accountant demand from the recipient implementing agencies the submission of liquidation/utilization reports, otherwise, apply the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of dormant receivables, if applicable, under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipality submit the appropriate legal documents to support the purchase of lot for the site of Ajuy National High School charged to the SEF to prove the propriety and validity of the transaction.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to prepare a schedule of unreleased checks as of year-end and provide the same to the Municipal Accountant;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to restore the unreleased checks as of year-end to the Cash in Bank-LCCA based on the schedule provided by the Municipal Treasurer; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a JEV to revert all stale checks identified in the Bank Reconciliation Statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the following:
Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare the necessary journal entries to record the transfer of unexpended balances of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Trust Liability and ensure the transfer of unexpended balance of the STF that is aged more than 5 years to the unappropriated surplus of the GF and thereafter, strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the accounting of unutilized LDRRMF balances to achieve fair presentation of the financial statements; and
Disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.
LDRRMO to:
Utilize the QRF strictly for implementation of projects and activities exclusively for relief and recovery programs as stated in RA No. 10121; and
Prepare and submit the following to the Office of the Auditor:
Monthly report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
LDRRMFIP in accordance with the prescribed format and content of the provisions of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the necessary adjustments to correct the balance of the affected accounts in the financial statements, and henceforth, ensure compliance to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the accounting of unutilized LDRRMF balances to ensure fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that funds received are supported with duly signed MOA and related documents, and to properly account and monitor the funds and their respective utilization and maintain a record of each fund received.

We also recommended and the concerned officials agreed to make certain that the trust receipts are used solely for the specific purpose for which it was created or the funds were received. Thus, the Audit Team was precluded from verifying the propriety of expenditures due to the non-submission of the corresponding copies of MOA.

We likewise recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile the erroneous entries affecting the Due to LGU account, and effect the necessary adjustments to ensure the correct and reliable presentation of the year-end balances in the financial statements.

We recommended and the OIC – Municipal Treasurer’s Office agreed to ascertain the status of the savings account maintained by the Municipality to determine its proper treatment.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the BRS using the adjusted balance method to properly reflect all items requiring adjustments to both the books and bank balances of the Cash in Bank account, and prepare the appropriate adjusting entries to record checks issued which became stale, so as to present the correct Cash in Bank balance in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer and the concerned offices agreed to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through the implementation of the identified priority development projects in compliance with Section 3.2 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1.

We also recommended and Management agreed to observe proper coordination and evaluate the necessity of the identified priority PPAs which to date, remained unimplemented otherwise, reprogram the same to the much-needed PPAs in accordance with Item 3.2 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, and ensure that all projects to be implemented during the year should be procurement-and-implementation ready.

We likewise recommended and the concerned offices agreed to ensure that all charges against the fund are in accordance with the approved AIP, and to prepare and submit quarterly, the Utilization Report of the 20% DF as required under Item 6 of the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, to enable reconciliation of expenditure items with the records of the different offices and to produce an accurate and reliable report at year-end.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that all disbursement vouchers are supported with complete documents and to submit to the Office of the Auditor the lacking documents enumerated in Annex J of this audit observation to enable the timely audit of transactions.

We recommended and the Officer in Charge of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office agreed to monitor and maintain a comprehensive report on the collection of real property taxes due for the current year, design and implement more effective collection strategies to maximize collection efficiency, and enforce remedies provided under Sections 256 to 260 of RA No. 7160 in the collection of delinquent RPT/SET.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

Ensure that only transactions with valid claims and covered with perfected contracts and documents evidencing receipt of goods and services are recognized as Accounts Payable in the books of accounts; and

Establish a sound system of internal control in the recording and monitoring of Accounts Payable to make certain it is properly recognized in the books, and the balances are correctly presented in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct all officials and employees to henceforth secure the approval of proper authorities regarding their official travels pursuant to Executive Order No. 77 and Republic Act No. 7160.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that all travel claims are duly authorized by proper officials and supported with complete documentation prior to the approval of disbursements and upon liquidation, in case of cash advances.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the municipal employees to properly schedule and adequately plan their work assignment and activities and discontinue approving requests to render overtime for particular works were by nature, can be considered regular routine works that can be done in regular working hours, otherwise, disallowance will be imposed on the succeeding claim of overtime services.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials and employees to take immediate actions to ensure the full utilization of the allocated budget and propose plans/ programs for GAD, PWDs and the Youth to maximize the funds and benefits thereof and ensure that the relevant LGU PPAs and GAD activities will address/respond to the gender issues identified.

We recommended that the LCE and other officials/personnel responsible:

Comply with the prescribed general policies and procedures in the implementation of the ADM Program as provided in the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-3, and ensure that all concerned personnel are able to abide with the prescribed processes and be accountable for the overall implementation of the program; and

Make written communications to the proponent agency on matters pertaining to the release of the requested funds of the SALINTUBIG program and facilitate the submission of the required documents to expedite the release and avoid cancellation of expected funds.

We recommended and Management agreed for:

The Municipal Accountant to facilitate the preparation and maintenance of a complete set of PPE Ledger Cards and reconcile the same with the Property Cards of the General Services Office, in accordance with pertinent provisions of the Handbook on Property and Supply Management System; and

The GSO and the Inventory Committee to conscientiously prepare the RPCPPE, and the Municipal Accountant to properly classify and record PPEs of the Municipality in the books to ensure that the figures and information provided therein are accurate and reliable.

Xxx

The Municipality consider availing the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 to finally establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended and Management agreed:

To create a Disposal Committee to determine the serviceability of PPEs and facilitate the disposal process of the unserviceable properties; and

For the Disposal Committee to prepare the Waste Material Report (WMR) and the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) in consonance with Section 125 of MNGAS Volume I and follow the disposal procedures in accordance with pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 22, 1992.

We recommended and Management has agreed that:

   The Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the CY 2022 BRS for the savings account maintained by the Municipality and to henceforth comply with pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 in the preparation and submission BRS to the Office of the Auditor;

2.	The OIC – Office of the Municipal Treasurer locate the supporting documents for the identified stale checks and present the same to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of the appropriate adjusting entries to correctly present the Cash in Bank balance in the financial statements; and

3.	The Municipal Accountant and the OIC – Office of the Municipal Treasurer make periodic reconciliation of the cashbooks and accounting records so that the cash in bank balances are accurately presented in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and OIC – Office of the Municipal Treasurer agreed to ensure the completeness of reports before the same is submitted for audit, cause the immediate submission of the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers with supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor, and to henceforth strictly adhere to the timeframes on the rendition of accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 and other pertinent issuances.

We recommended and the Acting Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile the balances appearing in their respective reports to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the balances reflected in the Financial Statements.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly prepare the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2016-004 and 2012-002 and make certain the propriety and completeness of required disclosures in order to achieve fair presentation of financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed for the:

1.	GSO to ensure that issuance of supplies and materials are supported with Requisition and Issue Slip, and prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued to the Municipal Accountant for the recording of expense;

2.	Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventories semestrally to determine the undistributed/unconsumed items, prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories, and reconcile the same to the recorded inventories of the Accounting Office; and

3.	Municipal Accountant to ensure that the recognition of the expenses relative to the issuance of inventories are duly supported with Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued accompanied by Requisition and Issue Slips.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

a)	Ensure that only transactions with valid claims and covered with perfected contracts and documents evidencing receipt of goods and services are recognized as Accounts Payable in the books of accounts;

b)	Conduct an analysis of the Accounts Payable and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correctly recognize the erroneous recordings made in its appropriate liability accounts; and



c)	Establish a sound system of internal control in the recording and monitoring of Accounts Payable to make certain it is properly recognized in the books, and the balances are correctly presented in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and OIC of Municipal Treasurer’s Office agreed to secure a copy of the Certificate of Time Deposits and documents evidencing that prior authority and approval from the Sangguniang Bayan and the Local Chief Executive was taken, including other documents to prove the existence of the investment to time deposits, and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor for review.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Engineer and the end-user units to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund thru the implementation of the identified priority development projects in compliance with Section 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, and to oversee and closely monitor the timely implementation of these projects.

We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Development Council and end user units to evaluate the necessity of the identified priority PPAs which to date, remained unimplemented otherwise, reprogram the same to much needed PPAs in accordance with Item 3.2 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1; and to henceforth ensure that all projects to be implemented during the year be included in the APP and should be procurement-and-implementation ready to ensure its timely implementation and completion.

We recommended and Management has agreed to direct the BAC Chairman and her concerned personnel to submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor, the lacking documents to support the negotiated procurement of various ancillary supplies for the COVID-19 vaccination program and henceforth, to adhere strictly with the provisions of RA No. 9184 in the conduct of its procurement activities.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the fund transfers received from source agencies are supported with duly signed Memorandum of Agreement and related documents, before allowing any disbursements and make certain that funds are utilized only for its intended purpose.

We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Focal Person/Project Proponent Department Heads to implement immediately without delay the funded programs of various LGUs and NGAs to attain the program’s objectives.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to ensure that a sound system of internal control is in place in the recording and accounting of fund transfers and direct the Municipal Accountant to properly account and monitor the funds and their respective utilization and maintain a record of each funds received.

We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to perform periodic reconciliation of funds received to ensure completeness of recorded receipts and to ascertain the status of funds.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Officer-in-Charge of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prepare the certified list of RPT and SET due and collectible for the year based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Index Card from the Assessor’s Office and submit the same to Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year for the setting up of receivables.

We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to henceforth recognize in the books the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based solely on the certified list of due and collectible real property taxes as submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

We recommended and the Officer-in-Charge of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office agreed to design and implement more effective collection strategies to maximize collection efficiency, and enforce remedies provided under Sections 256 to 260 of RA No. 7160 in the collection of delinquent RPT/SET.

We recommended and the OIC - Office of the Municipal Treasurer agreed to intensify its collection efforts for the remaining uncollected market stall fees as of December 31, 2022, and to henceforth ensure that all rentals are supported by a valid and binding lease contract which should contain the following terms and conditions, among others: the nature and duration of the lease, rental rate, and rights/obligations of each party, copy furnished the COA Office.

We recommended and the Acting Budget Officer agreed to maintain separate registries for the LDRRMF to properly monitor its utilization and any other movements therein, and to facilitate the efficient determination of the balances of the LDRRM Fund for current and prior years.

We also recommended and the Acting Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct periodic reconciliation of the balances of the LDRRMF to ensure the correctness and reliability of reports submitted.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to transfer intact the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF for CYs 2018-2022 to the Special Trust Fund and to henceforth strictly observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 so as to avoid misuse of the fund.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council review and update the LDRRMFIP annually and include under a separate caption, the list of specific projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of prior years and incorporate the same in the AIP of the Municipality. 

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make certain that PPAs are included in the AIP prior to the processing of disbursements and ensure the proper recognition of procured DRR equipment in the books.

Lastly, we reiterated our previous recommendation and Management agreed that the MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the Monthly and Annual Report on Receipts and Utilization of the DRRM Fund in the prescribed format as required in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No 2013-1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and Management agreed that the MDRRMO and other concerned officials reassess all PPAs included in its current and continuing appropriations which remained unimplemented to date and determine the necessity to implement the same otherwise, reprogram to priority PPAs and ensure its full implementation in accordance with the guidelines set under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73 in order to strengthen the Municipality’s disaster risk reduction and management capability.

We recommended and Management agreed to henceforth ensure that all Variation Orders undergo careful evaluation and ensure that the same shall be approved only in accordance with the pertinent provisions of Annex E, of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and the concerned offices agreed to henceforth adhere to the provisions prescribed in the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. No. 9184 as amended by GPPB Resolution No. 30-2017 pertaining to warranty security for the procurement of goods and ensure that payments are either deducted with the 1 percent retention money or required with a special bank guarantee of the same amount, so that the municipal government is covered with a financial guarantee in case  defects are noted within the warranty period.

We recommended and the GAD Focal Person agreed to prepare the Municipality’s GAD Agenda for the three-year period as required under Item C2 par. 1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to establish the Municipality’s strategic framework and plan on gender mainstreaming, women empowerment and gender equality; and prepare the annual GPB in accordance with the GAD Agenda to ensure that identified programs and projects will answer GAD issues that directly relates to the GAD goals of the Municipality for the year.

We also recommended and the GAD Focal Person agreed to henceforth comply with the requirements of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 in the preparation of the GPB and GAD AR, ensure correctness of reported figures and information in both reports, and incorporate therein the reason for any deviation/variance to enable proper review and evaluation of GAD accomplishments of the Municipality vis-à-vis the GAD Plan and Budget.

We further recommended and the GAD Focal Person, the Municipal Accountant and other concerned offices agreed to establish a sound system of monitoring in the recording of expenditures and utilization of the Fund and make periodic reconciliation to ensure correctness of reports submitted.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct all officials and employees to henceforth secure the approval of proper authorities regarding their official travels pursuant to Executive Order No. 77 and Republic Act No. 7160.




We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that all travel claims are duly authorized by proper officials and supported with complete documentation prior to the approval of disbursements and upon liquidation, in case of cash advances.

Require the previous Municipal Treasurer to produce the undeposited collections and deposit the same with the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB) and provide explanation why the said amount was undeposited as of December 31, 2021 or if necessary, initiate proper proceedings; and

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS monthly to the Auditor, within ten (10) days after receipt of the BS.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant secure print-out or snap-shot copy of the BS as an alternative procedure in preparing the BRS on time.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the LCE direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s properties, prepare the inventory reports of all PPE and submit said reports to the Auditor, Municipal Accountant and GSO.
We also recommended that the inventory reports be reconciled with the accounting records in order to present accurate and reliable PPE balances in the financial statement.  Further, the Municipal Accountant and GSO prepare and update the PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for proper identification of all PPE.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the LCE, as Chairman, and all the other members of the Municipal Planning and Development Council, observe the guidelines of the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 in formulating PPAs to be funded out of the 20% DF, to include specific and identifiable PPAs which are attainable within the desired time frame.  By this, the Municipality may be able to maximize the purpose of the 20% DF which will have an immediate impact on the socio-economic development of the Municipality.
We also recommended that the fund be strictly utilized according to the guidelines in order to achieve the maximum benefits for its constituents.

Recommendation:

Recommendations:

Recommendations:

The budget shall be supported by PPAs, and Resolution, in prescribed format under the Budget Operations Manual for Local Government Units.
Further, we recommended that the LSB focus the utilization of the SEF in providing adequate facilities and resources in the delivery of quality education by public elementary and secondary schools in the Municipality.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned officials and employees, to liquidate and/or refund the unused cash advances within the prescribed period pursuant to the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular Nos. 96-004, 97-002 and 2012-004, to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances and to attain a fair presentation of the relevant accounts in the financial statements. We also recommended that no additional cash advance be granted to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated pursuant to the aforecited provisions of law and regulations of COA.

Recommendations:

We also recommended that the LCE direct the personnel concerned to prepare the GPB and GAD AR following the instructions and format prescribed under the aforecited JMC, and ensure that all the information/data required are provided.

Recommendation:
